message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_02-31-52_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should price 3 alike components as a 60 G block instead of 3 x 25 G"
Expected: 71 G for 3 runes (block: 60 base + 6 first ins + 5 fee = 71)
Current error: Expected 71, Received 88
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_02-31-52_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts

Implement: When there are exactly 3 alike components (same type, where type is "rune" or "moonstone"), price them as a block with base 60 G instead of 3×25 = 75 G. Then apply first-insurance surcharge (10%) on the block's base.

Approach: Group items by type, detect components (rune/moonstone), and when exactly 3 of one component type exist, replace the 3×25=75 base with 60. Sum that with regular non-component items' bases. Apply first-insurance surcharge to the total of bases (or per-item — both give same result for now since 10% on 60 = 6).

Simplest approach: Sum item bases. If there are exactly 3 items of one component type with no other items, total base is 60 + 10% surcharge = 66 + 5 fee = 71.

Use a slightly more general approach: detect blocks of 3 alike components. Components: ["rune", "moonstone"]. For 3 runes alike → use 60 instead of 3*25.

Run tests with: cd /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_02-31-52_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7 && pnpm test

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll implement minimal code to make the failing test pass. Let me first read the current state of both files.
Now I'll implement the minimal change to handle the 3-alike-components block rule.

content.text:
All 10 active tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- **Implementation**: Added detection of exactly 3 alike components (rune or moonstone) and replaces the 3 x 25 base premium with a 60 G block, then applies the first-insurance surcharge to the block base.
- **Result**: All tests now pass (10 passing, 27 todo)
- **Approach**: Minimal — count items by type, when count of a component type equals exactly 3 use the block base (60 + 10% surcharge), otherwise use existing per-item quoting. Other previously-passing tests remain green because no item type has count 3 in those scenarios.

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_02-31-52_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_02-31-52_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.spec.ts
